int(x[0])*60+int(x[1]))\n# absolute completion time in minutes since day0\nmaint['t_completed'] = (maint['day']-1)*1440 + maint['completed_min']\nmaint = pd.read_csv('/app/production_jobs.csv')\nproducts = pd.read_csv('/app/products.csv')\n\nb = batches.merge(assign[['job_id','machine_id','start_time','end_time','sequence_on_machine']], 1\n    g = grp.copy()\n    g['maint_idx'] = idx\n    g['wo'] = np.where(idx>=0, mm['wo'].reindex(idx.clip(0)).values, None)\n    g['technician_id'] = np.where(idx>=0, mm['technician_id'].reindex(idx.clip(0)).values, None)\n    g['work_type'] = np.where(idx>=0, mm['work_type'].reindex(idx.clip(0)).values, None)\n    g['maint_day'] = np.where(idx>=0, mm['day'].reindex(idx.clip(0)).values, np.nan)\n    out.append(g)\nbb = pd.concat(out)\nbb.to_pickle('/tmp/analysis/linked.pkl')\nprint(\"batches with no prior maintenance:\", (bb.maint_idx<0).sum())\nrej = bb[bb.inspection_result=='REJECTED']\nprint(\"rejected batches:\", len(rej), \" with no prior maint:\", (rej.maint_idx<0).sum())\n\n# Distribution of rejections per maintenance interval (machine, wo)\nper_wo = rej[rej.maint_idx>=0].groupby(['machine_id','wo','technician_id','work_type']).agg(\n    n_rej=('batch_id','count'), products=('product_id', lambda s: sorted(set(s))))\nper_wo = per_wo.sort_values('n_rej', ascending=False)\nprint(per_wo.head(40).to_string())\nprint(\"\\nintervals with >=2 rejections:\", (per_wo.n_rej>=2).sum(), \"of\", len(per_wo))\nprint(\"\\nrej pct stats by interval size:\")\nprint(per_wo.n_rej.value_counts().sort_index())\nEOF\npython3 /tmp/analysis/link.py","description":"Link batches to maintenance intervals, count rejections per interval"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1726,"cache_read_input_tokens":26294,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1726,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":38,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b30c335-00d3-4b4a-84de-4cce28d33b6e","uuid":"0fcbcdb6-93ca-4927-b8b1-8e9f48b75de5","request_id":"req_011CbwKbY23YTG592YWaZFG3"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_017wDYcRCMWunYeWRCMqe3C6","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NLUuMaQ1kDUfYz3vxZQxTp","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /tmp/analysis/confound.py << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nbb = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/analysis/linked.pkl')\nbb = bb[bb.maint_idx>=0].copy()\nbb['rej'] = (bb.inspection_result=='REJECTED').astype(int)\nbb['margin'] = bb.rejected_pct / bb.quality_rejection_threshold_pct\n\njobs = pd.read_csv('/app/production_jobs.csv')\nbb = bb.merge(jobs[['job_id','job_difficulty','priority']], on='job_id')\ncond = pd.read_csv('/app/daily_factory_conditions.csv')\nbb = bb.merge(cond, left_on='production_day', right_on='day')\n\n# normalize rejected_pct per product (robust z)\nstats = bb.groupby('product_id')['rejected_pct'].agg(['median']).rename(columns={'median':'med'})\nmad = bb.groupby('product_id')['rejected_pct'].apply(lambda s: (s-s.median()).abs().median()).rename('mad')\nbb = bb.merge(stats, on='product_id').merge(mad, on='product_id')\nbb['z'] = (bb.rejected_pct - bb.med) / (1.4826*bb.mad)\n\nprint(\"difficulty effect on z:\", bb.groupby('job_difficulty')['z'].mean().to_dict())\nfor c in ['load_index','humidity_index','material_variation_index','power_stability_index']:\n    print(f\"corr(z, {c}) =\", np.corrcoef(bb.z, bb[c])[0,1].round(4))\n\nprint(\"\\nz-score distribution overall:\")\nprint(bb.z.describe())\nprint(\"\\nz for REJECTED batches:\")\nrej = bb[bb.rej==1]\nprint(np.histogram(rej.z, bins=[0,2,3,4,5,6,8,10,15,30]))\nprint(\"\\nz>5 rejected count:\", (rej.z>5).sum(), \" z in 3..5:\", ((rej.z>3)&(rej.z<=5)).sum())\nprint(\"\\nACCEPTED batches with z>5:\", (bb[(bb.rej==0)].z>5).sum())\nprint(\"ACCEPTED z>4:\", (bb[(bb.rej==0)].z>4).sum())\nbb.to_pickle('/tmp/analysis/full.pkl')\nEOF\npython3 /tmp/analysis/confound.py","description":"Test confounders and compute robust z-scores"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3638,"cache_read_input_tokens":33720,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3638,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":49,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b30c335-00d3-4b4a-84de-4cce28d33b6e","uuid":"8d1014d6-258a-4322-8441-9fe143a7eda2","request_id":"req_011CbwKgbKcyxLR34sGkukyv"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01PBf2ASdUWYw275GUnjjm1m","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0129snKFfVbNp6gMNeStQLPV","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /tmp/analysis/model.py << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nbb = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/analysis/full.pkl')\nprint(\"zero rejected_pct:\", (bb.rejected_pct==0).sum(), \"min nonzero:\", bb.loc[bb.rejected_pct>0,'rejected_pct'].min())\n\n# Fit natural model on ACCEPTED batches: log(rejected_pct) ~ product + difficulty + log(conditions)\nd = bb.copy()\nd['y'] = np.log(d.rejected_pct.clip(lower=1e-4))\nimport statsmodels.formula.api as smf\nacc = d[d.rej==0]\nmod = smf.ols('y ~ C(product_id) + C(job_difficulty) + np.log(load_index) + np.log(humidity_index) + np.log(material_variation_index) + np.log(power_stability_index)', data=acc).fit()\nprint(mod.params.tail(8))\nprint(\"R2:\", mod.rsquared.round(3), \"resid std:\", mod.resid.std().round(3))\nd['pred'] = mod.predict(d)\nd['res'] = (d.y - d.pred) / mod.resid.std()\nprint(\"\\nresidual z for ACCEPTED: max\", d.loc[d.rej==0,'res'].max().round(2), \" q999:\", d.loc[d.rej==0,'res'].quantile(0.999).round(2))\nprint(\"residual z for REJECTED:\")\nprint(np.histogram(d.loc[d.rej==1,'res'], bins=[-2,0,1,2,3,4,5,6,8,12]))\nd.to_pickle('/tmp/analysis/model.pkl')\nEOF\npython3 /tmp/analysis/model.py","description":"Fit natural rejection model and compute residuals"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3311,"cache_read_input_tokens":37358,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3311,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":61,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b30c335-00d3-4b4a-84de-4cce28d33b6e","uuid":"7b9d1630-94fc-4685-9504-5825588c61bd","request_id":"req_011CbwKjkrwHJwSPJa28RAEg"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01DUAjKFrwCBGp2f7qKisUPp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01D848xsoVvDQxt8HfCaRmsc","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /tmp/analysis/clusters.py << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nd = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/analysis/model.pkl')\n\nrej = d[d.rej==1]\n# candidate intervals: any rejected batch with res>3, or any (interval,product) with >=2 rejections\nstrong = rej[rej.res>3].groupby(['machine_id','wo']).size()\nmulti = rej.groupby(['machine_id','wo','product_id']).size()\nmulti = multi[multi>=2].reset_index().groupby(['machine_id','wo']).size()\ncands = sorted(set(strong.index) | set(multi.index))\nprint(f\"{len(cands)} candidate intervals\\n\")\nfor (m,wo) in cands:\n    iv = d[(d.machine_id==m)&(d.wo==wo)]\n    tech = iv.technician_id.iloc[0]; wt = iv.work_type.iloc[0]; mday = int(iv.maint_day.iloc[0])\n    print(f\"== {m} {wo} {tech} {wt} maint_day={mday} interval days {iv.production_day.min()}-{iv.production_day.max()}, {len(iv)} batches\")\n    g = iv.groupby('product_id').agg(n=('rej','size'), k=('rej','sum'))\n    g = g[g.k>0]\n    for p,row in g.iterrows():\n        rs = iv[(iv.product_id==p)]\n        det = \"; > /tmp/analysis/episodes.py << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nfrom scipy import stats as st\nd = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/analysis/model.pkl')\n\n# natural rate per product estimated from batches with res<2 ... rate = rejections that are \"natural-looking\"\n# conservative: use all batches but count only rejections with res<=3.3 (max accepted res) as natural\nnat = d.groupby('product_id').apply(lambda g: pd.Series({\n    'n': len(g), 'k_nat': ((g.rej==1)&(g.res<=3.3)).sum(), 'k_all': g.rej.sum()}), include_groups=False)\nnat['p_nat'] = nat.k_nat/nat.n\nprint(nat[nat.k_all>0].round(4).to_string())\n\ng = d[d.rej==1].groupby(['machine_id','wo','product_id'])\nrows=[]\nfor (m,wo,p), grp in g:\n    iv = d[(d.machine_id==m)&(d.wo==wo)&(d.product_id==p)]\n    n,k = len(iv), int(iv.rej.sum())\n    pn = nat.loc[p,'p_nat']\n    pval = st.binom.sf(k-1, n, max(pn,1e-4))\n    rows.append(dict(machine=m, wo=wo, product=p, tech=grp.technician_id.iloc[0],\n        maint_day=int(grp.maint_day.iloc[0]), n=n, k=k, p_binom=pval,\n        mean_res=grp.res.mean(), max_res=grp.res.max(), days=sorted(grp.production_day.unique())))\nep = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values(['machine','maint_day'])\nep['fault_score'] = -np.log10(ep.p_binom.clip(lower=1e-300)) + ep.max_res\npd.set_option('display.width',250)\nprint(\"\\nAll (interval,product) rejection groups sorted by machine/day:\")\nprint(ep.to_string(index=False, formatters={'p_binom':'{:.1e}'.format,'mean_res':'{:.1f}'.format,'max_res':'{:.1f}'.format}))\nep.to_pickle('/tmp/analysis/ep.pkl')\nEOF\npython3 /tmp/analysis/episodes.py 2>/dev/null | head {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01SLjpJq97FUSNj6pDXMeuux","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012DDrK54BtPUFQ4huZXzYyF","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /tmp/analysis/final_detect.py << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nd = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/analysis/model.pkl').sort_values('t_start')\nmaint = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/analysis/maint.pkl')\n\nANCHOR = 3.3   # max residual among 43k accepted batches is 3.26\nELEV   = 2.0\n\nepisodes = []  # dict: machine, product, batches(list of rows), anchored\nfor (m,p), g in d.groupby(['machine_id','product_id']):\n    g = g.sort_values('t_start')\n    elev = ((g.rej==1)&(g.res>=ELEV)) | (g.res>=ANCHOR)\n    run = []\n    for i, (_, r) in enumerate(g.iterrows()):\n        if elev.iloc[i]:\n            run.append(r)\n        else:\n            if run: episodes.append(dict(machine=m, product=p, rows=run)); run=[]\n    if run: episodes.append(dict(machine=m, product=p, rows=run))\n\n# anchored episodes\nanch = [e for e in episodes if any(r.res>=ANCHOR and r.rej==1 for r in e['rows'])]\n# cross-product coupling: weak episodes sharing an interval (machine,wo) with an anchored episode\nanchored_ivs = set((e['machine'], r.wo) for e in anch for r in e['rows'])\ncoupled = [e for e in episodes if e not in anch and any((e['machine'], r.wo) in anchored_ivs for r in e['rows'] if r.rej==1)]\nprint(f\"{len(anch)} anchored episodes, {len(coupled)} coupled weak episodes\\n\")\n\nmm_sorted = {m: g.sort_values('t_completed').reset_index(drop=True) for m,g in maint.groupby('machine_id')}\nout=[]\nfor e in anch+coupled:\n    m,p,rows = e['machine'], e['product'], e['rows']\n    first = rows[0]\n    start_wo = first.wo; tech = first.technician_id\n    # previous same-product batch before episode & maintenance gap diagnostics\n    g = d[(d.machine_id==m)&(d.product_id==p)&(d.t_start<first.t_start)]\n    prev = g.iloc[-1] if len(g) else None\n    mmach = mm_sorted[m]\n    wo_idx = {w:i for i,w in enumerate(mmach.wo)}\n    gap = wo_idx[start_wo] - (wo_idx[prev.wo] if prev is not None and pd.notna(prev.wo) else -1) - 1 if prev is not None else None\n    rej_rows = [r for r in rows if r.rej==1]\n    own = [r for r in rej_rows if r.wo==start_wo]\n    wos = sorted(set(r.wo for r in rej_rows))\n    kind = 'ANCH' if e in anch else 'coup'\n    out.append(dict(kind=kind, machine=m, product=p, tech=tech, start_wo=start_wo,\n        d0=int(first.production_day), d1=int(rows[-1].production_day),\n        n_rej=len(rej_rows), n_own=len(own), n_wos=len(wos),\n        prev_clean=f\"d{int(prev.production_day)} res{prev.res:+.1f}\" if prev is not None else \"none\",\n        wo_gap=gap, res_seq=\" \".join(f\"{r.res:.1f}{'R' if r.rej else 'a'}({r.technician_id[5:]})\" for r in rows)))\nep = pd.DataFrame(out).sort_values(['machine','d0'])\npd.set_option('display.width', 300)\nprint(ep.to_string(index=False))\nep.to_pickle('/tmp/analysis/final_ep.pkl')\n\nimport pickle\npickle.dump(anch+coupled, open('/tmp/analysis/episodes_raw.pkl','wb'))\nEOF\npython3 /tmp/analysis/final_detect.py","description":"Detect anchored fault episodes with chain-start diagnostics"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8424,"cache_read_input_tokens":79282,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":8424,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b30c335-00d3-4b4a-84de-4cce28d33b6e","uuid":"8db000a8-f629-47d1-a356-bdeecffbd73b","request_id":"req_011CbwL9rqmvnBiKTREa9WjP"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01GQhTqK1ddeSP2RXqEyKqGh","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_018UjuwBWAA5j2c6SAhzecoz","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat > /tmp/analysis/assemble.py << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, pickle, json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\nepisodes = pickle.load(open('/tmp/analysis/episodes_raw.pkl','rb'))\n\n# Each episode: chain-start WO = WO preceding first elevated batch; introducer = its technician.\n# Attribution (per task rule a-c): only REJECTED batches produced in the chain-start WO's own\n# interval are linked to the introducer. Carry-over batches in later intervals link to no one.\ntech_batches = defaultdict(set)   # tech -> set of batch_ids\ntech_wos     = defaultdict(set)   # tech -> set of introducing WOs with >=1 linked batch\ntech_prod    = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))\n\nfor e in episodes:\n    rows = e['rows']\n    start_wo, tech = rows[0].wo, rows[0].technician_id\n    own = [r for r in rows if r.rej==1 and r.wo==start_wo]\n    if not own: continue\n    for r in own:\n        tech_batches[tech].add(r.batch_id)\n        tech_wos[tech].add(start_wo)\n        tech_prod[tech][e['product']].append(r.batch_id)\n\nprint(f\"{'tech':9} {'batches':>7} {'WOs':>4}  status   products\")\nconfirmed = {}\nfor t in sorted(tech_batches):\n    nb, nw = len(tech_batches[t]), len(tech_wos[t])\n    ok = nb>=3 and nw>=2\n    prods = {p: len(v) for p,v in sorted(tech_prod[t].items())}\n    print(f\"{t:9} {nb:7} {nw:4}  {'CONFIRM' if ok else 'exclude'}  {prods}\")\n    if ok: confirmed[t] = sorted(tech_prod[t])\n\nans = defaultdict(list)\nfor t, prods in confirmed.items():\n    for p in prods: ans[p].append(t)\nanswer = {p: sorted(ts) for p, ts in sorted(ans.items())}\nprint(\"\\nFINAL ANSWER:\")\nprint(json.dumps(answer, indent=1))\njson.dump(answer, open('/app/answer.json','w'))\n\n# Cross-check: mechanical (latest-preceding-WO) variant for comparison\ntb2, tw2, tp2 = defaultdict(set), defaultdict(set), defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))\nfor e in episodes:\n    for r in e['rows']:\n        if r.rej==1:\n            tb2[r.technician_id].add(r.batch_id); tw2[r.technician_id].add(r.wo)\n            tp2[r.technician_id][e['product']].append(r.batch_id)\nprint(\"\\n[comparison] mechanical-linking variant:\")\nfor t in sorted(tb2):\n    nb, nw = len(tb2[t]), len(tw2[t])\n    if (nb>=3 and nw>=2) != (t in confirmed):\n        print(f\"  DIFFERS: {t} -> {nb} batches/{nw} WOs, products {sorted(tp2[t])}\")\nEOF\npython3 /tmp/analysis/assemble.py","description":"Assemble final attribution and write answer.json"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8923,"cache_read_input_tokens":125972,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":8923,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":63,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b30c335-00d3-4b4a-84de-4cce28d33b6e","uuid":"00e47a6f-e9ad-472d-b10d-e577fa59271e","request_id":"req_011CbwLsyeUA82jQCXMdBpYZ"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01WR7vGQL7B2aDcPyL5Zij5P","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Done — the answer is written to `/app/answer.json`. Seven technicians are confirmed as responsible for maintenance-caused rejections across ten products.\n\n## How I got there\n\n**Linking and modeling.** I parsed all 3,074 raw service reports (machine, technician, completion time), joined batches to their production start times, and linked each batch to the maintenance interval it was produced in. To rule out non-maintenance factors, I fit a model of natural rejection behavior per product, controlling for job difficulty and the daily factory conditions (load, material variation, power stability all measurably push rejection rates). This produced a clean separation: across 43,700 accepted batches the residual never exceeds 3.3, while fault-caused rejections sit at 3.3–7.2.\n\n**Fault episodes vs. noise.** Rejections fall into two clearly distinct populations:\n- *Natural*: scattered, barely over each product's threshold, concentrated in noise-prone products (P_020 alone has 87 such rejections, plus P_002, P_021, P_010, P_017, P_014) — these never show a high-residual episode and were excluded.\n- *Maintenance-caused*: abrupt, product-specific clusters that start immediately after one specific work order, reject essentially 100% of the affected product's batches (other products on the same machine unaffected — the \"fault class\" behavior), and stop only after a later maintenance event. 45 such episodes were found, e.g. machine M_028: P_004 fine through day 308, then 3 straight rejections at residual ~7 right after TECH_004's day-309 reset, persisting at decayed severity through two more work orders until fixed on day 317.\n\n**Attribution.** Faults demonstrably persist through maintenance that \"leaves the machine unchanged\" (e.g., M_015's P_024 fault spans four work orders, days 93–101). Per the linking rule, a batch is attributable only when produced *before the next maintenance event* — so carry-over rejections in later intervals can't be pinned on anyone, and each episode's introducing technician is credited only with rejections in their own work order's interval. I verified every chain start against a clean prior batch of the same product.\n\n**Confirmation thresholds.** Applying ≥3 linked rejected batches across ≥2 work orders confirmed **TECH_002, TECH_003, TECH_014, TECH_021, TECH_027, TECH_031, TECH_037** (TECH_014 is the worst: 25 batches across 9 botched work orders). Excluded as isolated cases despite real faults: TECH_010 (5 batches, one work order), TECH_004 (3 batches, one work order), TECH_030 (one botched job on M_009; its M_028 rejections are carry-over of TECH_004's fault), TECH_023 (2 batches across 2 work orders), and several single-batch cases — which also removes products P_011, P_016, and P_019 from the report, since their only rejections trace to unconfirmed technicians.\n\n```json\n{\"P_001\": [\"TECH_014\", \"TECH_027\", \"TECH_031\"], \"P_004\": [\"TECH_003\"], \"P_006\": [\"TECH_002\"],\n \"P_008\": [\"TECH_031\"], \"P_015\": [\"TECH_014\", \"TECH_021\"], \"P_018\": [\"TECH_014\"],\n \"P_024\": [\"TECH_003\", \"TECH_014\", \"TECH_037\"], \"P_026\": [\"TECH_014\", \"TECH_031\"],\n \"P_027\": [\"TECH_002\", \"TECH_021\"], \"P_029\": [\"TECH_002\", \"TECH_003\", \"TECH_014\", \"TECH_027\"]}\n```\n\nOne judgment call worth flagging: TECH_030 is the single case that hinges on interpretation. If carry-over batches were instead attributed mechanically to the most recent work order regardless of who introduced the fault, TECH_030 would qualify (adding P_019 and P_004 entries). I excluded them because the linking rule's condition (c) only has meaning as a filter against exactly this carry-over situation, and the severity-decay pattern on M_028 shows one persisting fault rather than fresh ones."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":689,"cache_read_input_tokens":138099,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":689,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6b30c335-00d3-4b4a-84de-4cce28d33b6e","uuid":"fc1a94bc-ac51-40ae-be8b-c57c91e95237","request_id":"req_011CbwLwK66fAsnUV9GVcr1j"}
